summary: This is a brief news post from Joel Spolsky sharing a quote from Benjamin Pollack about encountering an Internal Compiler Error when trying to build a project that claimed to work fine with Visual Studio .NET 2003. The post also includes a promotional reminder about FogBugz 4.0 introductory pricing ending that day.
key_points: Software that claims to 'build just fine' often doesn't, especially across different environments
Internal Compiler Errors represent the most frustrating class of build failures because they indicate bugs in the toolchain itself, not your code
The post doubles as a promotional reminder for FogBugz 4.0's introductory pricing deadline
money_quotes: I hit F5, the build starts, and then dies with my least favorite error message in the entire world: Internal Compiler Error.
key_insight: Even when documentation promises a smooth build experience, Internal Compiler Errors remain the most dreaded and uncontrollable failures a developer can encounter.
